actionUnits.condition: Automatic turn-on coverage: Ensure at least two neighboring Fire instances are within maxNeighborDistance (3.5–4) and currently isOn so that onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold (2). This will set shouldTurnOn = true and, after timeToOn (~40–60s set in Awake), Update() will set isOn = true, reset timers, and call UpdateFireState(true) which plays particles and notifies neighbors + FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1). Also try to include a down neighbor (> minNeighborDownDistance = 3 lower in Y) to exercise downMultiplier path.

Turn-off coverage via water hits: While isOn is true, repeatedly invoke Fire.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) at intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) so currentHitInterval is reset before expiring and currentHitTime accumulates to >= totalHitTime (5s). Expected result: isOn becomes false, timers reset, UpdateFireState(true) stops particles, notifies neighbors, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: This object has a BoxCollider but no Rigidbody; a separate water object with a Rigidbody or an event system must drive WaterHit calls.
